# github.gg

> AI-powered GitHub repository analysis platform

## Overview
github.gg provides AI-powered code analysis, developer profiles, repository insights, and documentation generation for GitHub repositories.

## Key Features
- Repository Analysis: Code quality analysis, architecture diagrams, and scorecards
- Developer Profiles: AI-generated developer scorecards and portfolios
- Wiki Generation: Automated documentation and wiki pages from code
- Code Arena: Compare and rank code solutions
- Developer Wrapped: Annual developer statistics and highlights

## Key Pages
- / - Homepage and dashboard
- /[user] - Developer profile page
- /[owner]/[repo] - Repository analysis page
- /[owner]/[repo]/diagram - Architecture diagrams
- /[owner]/[repo]/wiki - Generated wiki documentation
- /[owner]/[repo]/scorecard - Repository quality scorecard
- /arena - Code arena and leaderboard
- /pricing - Subscription plans

## API
- Repository analysis is triggered by visiting github.gg/[owner]/[repo]
- Developer profiles at github.gg/[username]
- Replace github.com URLs with github.gg to analyze any repository

## Data Schemas

### Developer Profile
- name: string - Display name of the developer
- bio: string - Short biography or tagline
- skills: string[] - List of programming languages and technologies
- repos: object[] - Top repositories with name, description, stars, language
- scores: object - Skill scores across categories (e.g., frontend, backend, devops)
- summary: string - AI-generated summary of the developer's work
- avatarUrl: string - GitHub avatar URL
- location: string - Developer location (if public)
- followers: number - GitHub follower count
- publicRepos: number - Number of public repositories

### Repository Analysis
- scorecard: object - Code quality metrics (maintainability, documentation, testing, security)
- diagram: object - Architecture diagram data (components, connections, layers)
- wiki: object[] - Generated documentation pages with title, content, category

## Analysis Types
- diagram: Architecture and component diagrams generated from code structure
- wiki: Automated documentation pages generated from source code
- scorecard: Repository quality scorecard with metrics for maintainability, documentation, testing, and security
- ai-slop: AI-generated code detection analysis
- pulls: Pull request listing and detail views
- issues: Issue listing and detail views

## Supported Languages and Frameworks
github.gg supports analysis of repositories in all major programming languages including:
- JavaScript / TypeScript (React, Next.js, Vue, Angular, Node.js)
- Python (Django, Flask, FastAPI)
- Java (Spring Boot, Maven, Gradle)
- Go
- Rust
- C / C++
- Ruby (Rails)
- PHP (Laravel)
- Swift
- Kotlin
- And many more

## Rate Limiting
- Unauthenticated users: Limited analysis requests per day
- Free tier: Standard analysis quota with basic features
- Pro tier: Higher analysis quota with advanced features
- Team tier: Unlimited analysis with team collaboration features

## Example URLs and Usage Patterns

### Analyze a repository
Replace github.com with github.gg in any GitHub URL:
- https://github.gg/facebook/react - Analyze the React repository
- https://github.gg/vercel/next.js - Analyze the Next.js repository

### View architecture diagrams
- https://github.gg/facebook/react/diagram

### View repository scorecard
- https://github.gg/facebook/react/scorecard

### View generated wiki
- https://github.gg/facebook/react/wiki

### View developer profiles
- https://github.gg/torvalds - Linus Torvalds' developer profile
- https://github.gg/gaearon - Dan Abramov's developer profile

### View pull requests
- https://github.gg/facebook/react/pulls - All pull requests
- https://github.gg/facebook/react/pulls/12345 - Specific PR

### View issues
- https://github.gg/facebook/react/issues - All issues
- https://github.gg/facebook/react/issues/12345 - Specific issue

## Contact
- Website: https://github.gg
- GitHub: https://github.com/nicepkg/github.gg
